text.title: structured-output-mode-picker Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure for picking the mode and producing a decision-record.

step.name: classify-consumer
step.input: One paragraph describing the stage's purpose.
step.action: Tag the consumer: `extraction` (typed parse), `action` (dispatch to function), `dsl` (SQL/regex/custom CFG), or `legacy` (free-form JSON downstream).

step.name: enumerate-available-modes
step.action: List which of the four modes is supported per provider; flag any provider that lacks strict SO so the fallback policy applies.

step.name: shortlist-and-eval
step.input: ≥2 candidate modes from steps 1 + 2; eval set with ≥10 rows.
step.action: Run the eval against each candidate. Record per-mode accuracy + cost + latency.
step.decision-gate: If the top two are within 1 absolute point, prefer the simpler mode (mode mismatch is a maintenance cost).

step.name: write-decision-record
step.action: Author `decision-record.md` per the `templates/decision-record.md` skeleton. Include `stage`, `consumer`, `chosen_mode`, `alternatives_considered`, `rationale` (≥24 chars), `eval_delta`.

step.name: validate-and-merge
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-structured-output-mode-picker.py record.json`. On PASS, open the migration PR linking the record; on FAIL, surface violations and fix.
step.decision-gate: If `chosen_mode == "json-mode"`, the record MUST link a tracking issue to migrate when strict SO is available.
